diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d193496
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+import { describe, expectTypeOf, test } from "vitest";
+import { filter } from "./filter";
+import { hasProp } from "./hasProp";
+import { pipe } from "./pipe";
+
+describe("dataFirst", () => {
+  test("returns a boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a")).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows an optional property to required", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: number };
+    if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a: number }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("keeps an explicit undefined in the value type", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a: number | undefined; b?: string };
+    if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a: number | undefined; b?: string }>();
+    }
+  });
+});
+
+describe("dataLast", () => {
+  test("returns a boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(
+      pipe({ a: 1 } as { a?: number }, hasProp("a")),
+    ).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows the elements kept by filter", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<
+      { a: number }[]
+    >();
+  });
+});
diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4205d99
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+import type { Simplify } from "type-fest";
+import { purry } from "./purry";
+
+// Narrows `T` so that `Key` is guaranteed to be a present, required property.
+// When `Key` is already part of `T` we mark just that key as required (keeping
+// its value type, including an explicit `undefined`); otherwise we add it with
+// an `unknown` value, since the runtime can only tell us that it exists.
+type WithRequiredProp<T, Key extends PropertyKey> = Simplify<
+  T & (Key extends keyof T ? Required<Pick<T, Key>> : Record<Key, unknown>)
+>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has its **own** property `key`, equivalent to
+ * `Object.hasOwn`. Inherited properties (e.g. those coming from the prototype
+ * chain) don't count; a property that exists but holds `undefined` does. When
+ * used as a type-guard the type of `data` is narrowed so that `key` is a
+ * required property.
+ *
+ * @param data - The object to test.
+ * @param key - The property key to look for.
+ * @returns `true` if `key` is an own property of `data`, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *    hasProp(data, key)
+ * @example
+ *    hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a"); //=> true
+ * @dataFirst
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<T extends object, Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  data: T,
+  key: Key,
+): data is WithRequiredProp<T, Key>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has its **own** property `key`, equivalent to
+ * `Object.hasOwn`. Inherited properties (e.g. those coming from the prototype
+ * chain) don't count; a property that exists but holds `undefined` does. When
+ * used as a type-guard the type of `data` is narrowed so that `key` is a
+ * required property.
+ *
+ * @param key - The property key to look for.
+ * @returns `true` if `key` is an own property of `data`, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *    hasProp(key)(data)
+ * @example
+ *    pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a")); //=> true
+ *    filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a")); // keeps the elements that own "a"
+ * @dataLast
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  key: Key,
+): <T extends object>(data: T) => data is WithRequiredProp<T, Key>;
+
+export function hasProp(...args: readonly unknown[]): unknown {
+  return purry(hasPropImplementation, args);
+}
+
+const hasPropImplementation = (data: object, key: PropertyKey): boolean =>
+  Object.hasOwn(data, key);

diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..56cd073
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,55 @@
+import { describe, expectTypeOf, test } from "vitest";
+import { filter } from "./filter";
+import { hasProp } from "./hasProp";
+import { pipe } from "./pipe";
+
+describe("data-first", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a")).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows an optional property to required", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: number };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data.a).toEqualTypeOf<number>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("adds a key that isn't part of the type", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a: number };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "b")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data.b).toEqualTypeOf<unknown>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("narrows symbol keys", () => {
+    const symbol = Symbol("test");
+    const data = {} as { [symbol]?: number };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, symbol)) {
+      expectTypeOf(data[symbol]).toEqualTypeOf<number>();
+    }
+  });
+});
+
+describe("data-last", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows as a stand-alone guard", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: number };
+
+    if (hasProp("a")(data)) {
+      expectTypeOf(data.a).toEqualTypeOf<number>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("narrows the elements kept by filter", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(
+      filter([] as Array<{ a?: number }>, hasProp("a")),
+    ).toEqualTypeOf<Array<{ a?: number } & Record<"a", unknown>>>();
+  });
+});
diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..9fbe27c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+import { purry } from "./purry";
+
+// The narrowed type when the guard passes: `T` with `key` guaranteed to be a
+// present, required property. For keys already in `T` we go through
+// `Required<Pick<...>>` rather than `Record<K, T[K]>` because, under
+// `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, indexed access of an optional property (`T[K]`)
+// widens to include `undefined`; the homomorphic `-?` mapping inside `Required`
+// strips both the optionality and that spurious `undefined`. For keys not in
+// `T` we add them as `unknown`. The leading `T &` keeps the predicate assignable
+// to the input type (a requirement for type predicates) and preserves the other
+// properties.
+type WithProp<T, K extends PropertyKey> = T &
+  (K extends keyof T ? Required<Pick<T, K>> : Record<K, unknown>);
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has `key` as an **own** property, i.e. a property defined
+ * directly on the object itself and not inherited from its prototype chain.
+ * This is the runtime equivalent of the built-in `Object.hasOwn`. A property
+ * whose value is `undefined` still counts as present (it returns `true`),
+ * whereas a missing optional property returns `false`. When used as a type-
+ * guard the result narrows `data` so that `key` is a required property.
+ *
+ * @param data - The object to check.
+ * @param key - The property key to look for. Can be a string, number, or symbol.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(data, key)
+ * @example
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a"); //=> true
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "b"); //=> false
+ * @dataFirst
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<T extends object, K extends PropertyKey>(
+  data: T,
+  key: K,
+): data is WithProp<T, K>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has `key` as an **own** property, i.e. a property defined
+ * directly on the object itself and not inherited from its prototype chain.
+ * This is the runtime equivalent of the built-in `Object.hasOwn`. A property
+ * whose value is `undefined` still counts as present (it returns `true`),
+ * whereas a missing optional property returns `false`. When used as a type-
+ * guard the result narrows `data` so that `key` is a required property.
+ *
+ * @param key - The property key to look for. Can be a string, number, or symbol.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(key)(data)
+ * @example
+ *   pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a")); //=> true
+ *   filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a")); // keeps the elements that own "a"
+ * @dataLast
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<K extends PropertyKey>(
+  key: K,
+): <T extends object>(data: T) => data is T & Record<K, unknown>;
+
+export function hasProp(...args: readonly unknown[]): unknown {
+  return purry(hasPropImplementation, args);
+}
+
+const hasPropImplementation = (data: object, key: PropertyKey): boolean =>
+  Object.hasOwn(data, key);

diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..49c7a6e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+import { describe, expectTypeOf, test } from "vitest";
+import { filter } from "./filter";
+import { hasProp } from "./hasProp";
+import { pipe } from "./pipe";
+
+describe("data-first", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a")).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows an optional property to required", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: number; b?: string };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toMatchObjectType<{ a: number; b?: string }>();
+    } else {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a?: number; b?: string }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("narrows symbol keys", () => {
+    const symbol = Symbol("symbol");
+    const data = {} as { [symbol]?: number };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, symbol)) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toMatchObjectType<{ [symbol]: number }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("only accepts objects as data", () => {
+    // @ts-expect-error [ts2769] -- primitives have no own properties.
+    hasProp("a", "length");
+  });
+});
+
+describe("data-last", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows the kept elements when used as a filter predicate", () => {
+    const result = filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a"));
+
+    expectTypeOf(result).items.toMatchObjectType<{ a: number }>();
+  });
+});
diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ffb6b1d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+import { purry } from "./purry";
+
+// Narrows `T` so that `Key` is guaranteed to be a present, required property.
+// When `Key` is already part of `T` we only need to strip its optionality;
+// otherwise we add it with an `unknown` value, since the guard only proves
+// presence, not the value's type.
+type WithProp<T, Key extends PropertyKey> = T &
+  (Key extends keyof T ? Required<Pick<T, Key>> : Record<Key, unknown>);
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has its own (non-inherited) property `key`. Properties
+ * inherited through the prototype chain (e.g. `toString`, or array methods like
+ * `push`) don't count; only properties that belong directly to the object do. A
+ * property that is present but whose value is `undefined` still counts as
+ * present. This is a wrapper around `Object.hasOwn` and shares its semantics.
+ *
+ * When used as a guard the type of `data` is narrowed so that `key` is a
+ * present, required property.
+ *
+ * @param data - The object to test.
+ * @param key - The property key to look for.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has its own property `key`, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(data, key)
+ * @example
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a"); //=> true
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "b"); //=> false
+ * @dataFirst
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<T extends object, Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  data: T,
+  key: Key,
+): data is WithProp<T, Key>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has its own (non-inherited) property `key`. Properties
+ * inherited through the prototype chain (e.g. `toString`, or array methods like
+ * `push`) don't count; only properties that belong directly to the object do. A
+ * property that is present but whose value is `undefined` still counts as
+ * present. This is a wrapper around `Object.hasOwn` and shares its semantics.
+ *
+ * When used as a guard the type of `data` is narrowed so that `key` is a
+ * present, required property.
+ *
+ * @param key - The property key to look for.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has its own property `key`, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(key)(data)
+ * @example
+ *   pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a")); //=> true
+ *   filter([{ a: 1 }] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a")); //=> [{ a: 1 }]
+ * @dataLast
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  key: Key,
+): <T extends object>(data: T) => data is WithProp<T, Key>;
+
+export function hasProp(...args: readonly unknown[]): unknown {
+  return purry(hasPropImplementation, args);
+}
+
+const hasPropImplementation = (data: object, key: PropertyKey): boolean =>
+  Object.hasOwn(data, key);

diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..5ed2a2e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+import { describe, expectTypeOf, test } from "vitest";
+import { filter } from "./filter";
+import { hasProp } from "./hasProp";
+import { pipe } from "./pipe";
+
+describe("data-first", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a")).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("accepts numeric and symbol keys", () => {
+    const symbol = Symbol("a");
+
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ 0: "a" }, 0)).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ [symbol]: 1 }, symbol)).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows an optional field to required", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: string; b?: number };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toMatchObjectType<{ a: string; b?: number }>();
+    } else {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a?: string; b?: number }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("adds a key the type doesn't declare", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a: number };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "b")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toMatchObjectType<{ a: number; b: unknown }>();
+    }
+  });
+});
+
+describe("data-last", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows an optional field to required", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: string; b?: number };
+    const guard = hasProp("a");
+
+    if (guard(data)) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toMatchObjectType<{ a: string; b?: number }>();
+    } else {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a?: string; b?: number }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("narrows the elements kept by filter", () => {
+    const data = [] as { a?: number }[];
+
+    expectTypeOf(filter(data, hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<
+      ({ a?: number } & { a: number })[]
+    >();
+  });
+});
diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..206e59a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@
+import { purry } from "./purry";
+
+// Narrows `T` so that `key` is guaranteed to be a present, required property: a
+// key `T` already declares (even an optional one) becomes required, while a key
+// `T` doesn't declare is added with an `unknown` value. `Pick` can only operate
+// on existing keys, hence the conditional fork for keys outside of `keyof T`.
+type HasPropGuard<T, K extends PropertyKey> = T &
+  (K extends keyof T ? Required<Pick<T, K>> : Record<K, unknown>);
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has `key` as an **own** property, regardless of its value (a
+ * property explicitly set to `undefined` still counts as present). Properties
+ * inherited through the prototype chain are not considered. This is a typed
+ * wrapper around the built-in `Object.hasOwn` that, when used as a type-guard,
+ * narrows `data` so that `key` is a present (required) property.
+ *
+ * @param data - The object to check.
+ * @param key - The property key to look for. Could be a string, number, or
+ * symbol.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(data, key)
+ * @example
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a"); //=> true
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "b"); //=> false
+ * @dataFirst
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<T extends object, K extends PropertyKey>(
+  data: T,
+  key: K,
+): data is HasPropGuard<T, K>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has `key` as an **own** property, regardless of its value (a
+ * property explicitly set to `undefined` still counts as present). Properties
+ * inherited through the prototype chain are not considered. This is a typed
+ * wrapper around the built-in `Object.hasOwn` that, when used as a type-guard,
+ * narrows `data` so that `key` is a present (required) property.
+ *
+ * @param key - The property key to look for. Could be a string, number, or
+ * symbol.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(key)(data)
+ * @example
+ *   pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a")); //=> true
+ *   filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a")); // keeps elements that own "a"
+ * @dataLast
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<K extends PropertyKey>(
+  key: K,
+): <T extends object>(data: T) => data is HasPropGuard<T, K>;
+
+export function hasProp(...args: readonly unknown[]): unknown {
+  return purry(hasPropImplementation, args);
+}
+
+const hasPropImplementation = (data: object, key: PropertyKey): boolean =>
+  Object.hasOwn(data, key);

diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2483573
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@
+import { describe, expectTypeOf, test } from "vitest";
+import { filter } from "./filter";
+import { hasProp } from "./hasProp";
+import { pipe } from "./pipe";
+
+describe("data-first", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a")).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows an optional field to required", () => {
+    const data = {} as { a?: number; b?: string };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a: number; b?: string }>();
+    } else {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a?: number; b?: string }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("adds keys the type doesn't know about as unknown", () => {
+    const data = { a: 1 };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, "b")) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ a: number; b: unknown }>();
+    }
+  });
+
+  test("works with symbol and numeric keys", () => {
+    const symbol = Symbol("s");
+    const data = {} as { [symbol]?: number; 0?: string };
+
+    if (hasProp(data, symbol)) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ [symbol]: number; 0?: string }>();
+    }
+
+    if (hasProp(data, 0)) {
+      expectTypeOf(data).toEqualTypeOf<{ [symbol]?: number; 0: string }>();
+    }
+  });
+});
+
+describe("data-last", () => {
+  test("returns boolean", () => {
+    expectTypeOf(pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+  });
+
+  test("narrows the elements of a filtered array", () => {
+    const result = filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a"));
+
+    expectTypeOf(result).toEqualTypeOf<{ a: number }[]>();
+  });
+});
diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..6cc08ab
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+import type { Simplify } from "type-fest";
+import { purry } from "./purry";
+
+// Narrows `T` so that `Key` is guaranteed to be a present (required) property.
+// Keys the object already knows about are made required while keeping their
+// value type (intersecting `Required<Pick<…>>` drops the optionality and the
+// `undefined` it implies); keys the type doesn't know about (e.g. a wider
+// `PropertyKey`) are added as `unknown` since the runtime check only tells us
+// the property exists, not what it holds.
+type WithProp<T, Key extends PropertyKey> = Simplify<
+  T & Required<Pick<T, Key & keyof T>> & Record<Exclude<Key, keyof T>, unknown>
+>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has `key` as an **own** property, returning `true` even when
+ * the value stored at that key is `undefined`. Inherited properties (e.g.
+ * `toString`, or `Array.prototype.push`) are not considered. This is a thin,
+ * typed wrapper around the runtime `Object.hasOwn`.
+ *
+ * Acts as a type-guard: when it returns `true` the object is narrowed so that
+ * `key` is a present, required property.
+ *
+ * @param data - The object to check.
+ * @param key - The property key to look for.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(data, key)
+ * @example
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a"); //=> true
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "b"); //=> false
+ * @dataFirst
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<T extends object, Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  data: T,
+  key: Key,
+): data is WithProp<T, Key>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks if `data` has `key` as an **own** property, returning `true` even when
+ * the value stored at that key is `undefined`. Inherited properties (e.g.
+ * `toString`, or `Array.prototype.push`) are not considered. This is a thin,
+ * typed wrapper around the runtime `Object.hasOwn`.
+ *
+ * Acts as a type-guard: when it returns `true` the object is narrowed so that
+ * `key` is a present, required property.
+ *
+ * @param key - The property key to look for.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(key)(data)
+ * @example
+ *   pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a")); //=> true
+ *   filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a")); // keeps the elements that own "a"
+ * @dataLast
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  key: Key,
+): <T extends object>(data: T) => data is WithProp<T, Key>;
+
+export function hasProp(...args: readonly unknown[]): unknown {
+  return purry(hasPropImplementation, args);
+}
+
+const hasPropImplementation = (data: object, key: PropertyKey): boolean =>
+  Object.hasOwn(data, key);

diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a92f831
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.test-d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+import { expectTypeOf, test } from "vitest";
+import { filter } from "./filter";
+import { hasProp } from "./hasProp";
+import { pipe } from "./pipe";
+
+test("returns a boolean (data-first)", () => {
+  expectTypeOf(hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a")).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+});
+
+test("returns a boolean (data-last)", () => {
+  expectTypeOf(pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a"))).toEqualTypeOf<boolean>();
+});
+
+test("narrows an optional property to required", () => {
+  const data = {} as { a?: number };
+  if (hasProp(data, "a")) {
+    expectTypeOf(data.a).toEqualTypeOf<number>();
+  }
+});
+
+test("narrows symbol keys", () => {
+  const key = Symbol("key");
+  const data = {} as { [key]?: number };
+  if (hasProp(data, key)) {
+    expectTypeOf(data[key]).toEqualTypeOf<number>();
+  }
+});
+
+test("narrows the element type when used in filter", () => {
+  const data = filter([] as { a?: number }[], hasProp("a"));
+
+  expectTypeOf(data).toExtend<readonly { a: number }[]>();
+});
diff --git a/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..54e9b60
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/remeda/src/hasProp.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+import { purry } from "./purry";
+
+// When `Key` is already part of `T`'s type we only need to strip its
+// optionality to mark it as guaranteed-present; otherwise the key isn't known
+// to `T` yet, so we add it with an `unknown` value.
+type HasProp<T, Key extends PropertyKey> = T &
+  (Key extends keyof T ? Required<Pick<T, Key>> : Record<Key, unknown>);
+
+/**
+ * Checks whether `data` has `key` as its own (i.e. non-inherited) property.
+ * Inherited properties from the prototype chain (e.g. `toString`, or an
+ * array's `push`) don't count. A property that is present but whose value is
+ * `undefined` is still considered present. Works for string, numeric, and
+ * symbol keys, and for objects created with a `null` prototype.
+ *
+ * When used as a type-guard the type of `data` is narrowed so that `key` is a
+ * required, present property.
+ *
+ * @param data - The object to check.
+ * @param key - The key of the property to check for.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(data, key)
+ * @example
+ *   hasProp({ a: 1 }, "a"); //=> true
+ *   hasProp({ a: undefined }, "a"); //=> true
+ *   hasProp({} as { a?: number }, "a"); //=> false
+ * @dataFirst
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<T extends object, Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  data: T,
+  key: Key,
+): data is HasProp<T, Key>;
+
+/**
+ * Checks whether `data` has `key` as its own (i.e. non-inherited) property.
+ * Inherited properties from the prototype chain (e.g. `toString`, or an
+ * array's `push`) don't count. A property that is present but whose value is
+ * `undefined` is still considered present. Works for string, numeric, and
+ * symbol keys, and for objects created with a `null` prototype.
+ *
+ * When used as a type-guard the type of `data` is narrowed so that `key` is a
+ * required, present property.
+ *
+ * @param key - The key of the property to check for.
+ * @returns `true` if `data` has `key` as an own property, `false` otherwise.
+ * @signature
+ *   hasProp(key)(data)
+ * @example
+ *   pipe({ a: 1 }, hasProp("a")); //=> true
+ *   filter([{ a: 1 }, {} as { a?: number }], hasProp("a")); //=> [{ a: 1 }]
+ * @dataLast
+ * @category Guard
+ */
+export function hasProp<Key extends PropertyKey>(
+  key: Key,
+): <T extends object>(data: T) => data is HasProp<T, Key>;
+
+export function hasProp(...args: readonly unknown[]): unknown {
+  return purry(hasPropImplementation, args);
+}
+
+const hasPropImplementation = (data: object, key: PropertyKey): boolean =>
+  Object.hasOwn(data, key);
